issue_title: Theme resources are not loaded correctlyusing quarking when having multiple providers
issue_body:
### Describe the bug

When having multiple providers delivering themes resources containing messages only the first 'seen' provider get its messages loaded, all other providers are ignored. This seems to be related to the quarkus build configuration where only a single message bundle gets processed. 


### Version

17.0.1

### Expected behavior

Load message bundles from all providers

### Actual behavior

Only a single message bundle gets loaded (the first provider seen by the quarkus build)

### How to Reproduce?

Create 2 e.g. authentication providers each containing a theme-resources folder
First provider: 
Add template folder and add template-1.ftl
Add message folder and add messages_en.properties
Implement authentication provider and factory running a form challenge rendering template-1
Build and copy jar to keycloak providers  directory

Second provider:
Add template folder and add template-2.ftl 
Add message folder and add messages_en.properties
Implement authentication provider and factory running a form challenge rendering template-2
Build and copy jar to keycloak providers directory 

Start keycloak  (./kc.[bat|sh] start-dev)
Configure the browser flow authentication flow containing an execution on provider 1
Try login
Messages from the message bundle may be shown properly or the message key is visible (issue triggered, stop here)
Change the browser flow authentication removing provider 1 and adding provider 2
Try login
Messages from the message bundle are shown by their message key.




### Anything else?

_No response_

issue_title: Suppress Snyk false positives
issue_body:
### Description

Snyk reported 2 false positives and 1 alert that's not possible to fix: [SNYK-JAVA-ORGKEYCLOAK-1062507](https://security.snyk.io/vuln/SNYK-JAVA-ORGKEYCLOAK-1062507), [https://security.snyk.io/vuln/SNYK-JAVA-ORGKEYCLOAK-1088339](https://security.snyk.io/vuln/SNYK-JAVA-ORGKEYCLOAK-1088339) and [SNYK-JAVA-IONETTY-1042268](https://security.snyk.io/vuln/SNYK-JAVA-IONETTY-1042268).

We should be able to suppress those alerts to reduce the noise. More details below:

- **SNYK-JAVA-ORGKEYCLOAK-1062507**: The Keycloak core module is not affected by Open Redirect Vulnerability (CVE-2020-1723), that relates to Gatekeeper, an old project already decommissioned from our org. _I already requested the Snyk team to review this issue and if possible, remove from their database._

More details:
            - https://issues.redhat.com/browse/KEYCLOAK-11318
            - https://www.keycloak.org/2020/08/sunsetting-louketo-project.adoc
            - https://hub.docker.com/r/keycloak/keycloak-gatekeeper

- **SNYK-JAVA-ORGKEYCLOAK-1088339**: The Keycloak services module is not affected by CVE-2021-3461 anymore, the issue was fixed on Keycloak 14.0.0 last year. _I already requested the Snyk team to review this issue and if possible, remove from their database._ 

More details:
            - https://issues.redhat.com/browse/KEYCLOAK-17495

- **SNYK-JAVA-IONETTY-1042268**: There is no fixed version for io.netty:netty-handler. More details:
            - https://github.com/netty/netty/issues/10806
            - https://github.com/netty/netty/issues/8537
            - https://github.com/netty/netty/issues/9930
            - https://github.com/netty/netty/issues/10362

Netty Handler is a transitive dependency coming from Quarkus, according to the Netty team the fix should be available on Netty 5.
commit_message:
Suppress Snyk false positives

Resolves #11203

issue_title: Jpa Map Storage: break 1:1 relationship between JPA entity transactions and Jpa*MapKeycloakTransactions
issue_body:
### Describe the bug

Currently all transactions in the JPA storage create their own JPA transaction via `EntityManager`. This leads to problematic situations such as the one described in #11176.

We need to depart from this 1:1 relationship that currently exists between the `Jpa*MapKeycloakTransaction`s and the underlying JPA transaction. Ideally, areas that rely on the same JPA configuration (i.e. rely on the same `JpaStorageProviderFactory`) should operate on the same JPA transaction in a particular session. The linked discussion contains further information on how that should be achieved.

### Version

17.0.1

### Expected behavior

All JPA providers that use the same config (i.e. connect to the same DB instace) should participate in the same JPA transaction instead of creating a separate JPA transaction every time a provider is created.

### Actual behavior

We have a 1:1 relationship between the JPA transaction and the `Jpa*MapKeycloakTransaction`s, so providers are not participating in the same overall JPA transaction.

### How to Reproduce?

_No response_

### Anything else?

_No response_

Enlist JPA transaction in JpaMapStorageProvider.getStorage

Closes #11230

Co-authored-by: Alexander Schwartz <aschwart@redhat.com>

issue_title: Advanced Claim to Group Mapper resolves group by path rather than ID
issue_body:
### Describe the bug

#8467 implemented an OIDC provider mapper along with the frontend logic necessary for selecting a group. However, it stores the group reference as a serialized path rather than by an ID. This is problematic because group names are neither stable nor unique.

### Version

17.0.1

### Expected behavior

The mapper should store a group by its ID and continue to function after a group is renamed. This aligns with how Group polices are stored and evaluated.

### Actual behavior

The user is assigned to the first occurrence of a group with a matching name, and error is thrown when the group is renamed or moved somewhere else in the hierarchy. 

There's additionally an unintentional null pointer exception due to an additional bug where the `IdentityBrokerException` should be thrown:
https://github.com/keycloak/keycloak/blob/bde274465096bf04e65ec07a21fdea7b73efe921/services/src/main/java/org/keycloak/broker/oidc/mappers/AbstractClaimToGroupMapper.java#L83-L85

### How to Reproduce?

_No response_

### Anything else?

It seems like groups are only ever resolved by path when importing groups externally, i.e. where the external name informs the name of the group on Keycloak. Since this mapper explicitly configures a target group from a picker UI, I don't think this behavior is appropriate.
commit_message:
Avoid login failures in case of non-existing group or role references and update references in case of renaming or moving

- no longer throw an exception, when a role or group cannot be found, log a warning instead
- update mapper references in case of the following events:
   - moving a group
   - renaming a group
   - renaming a role
   - renaming a client's Client ID (may affect role qualifiers)
- in case a role or group is removed, the reference still will not be changed
- extend and refactor integration tests in order to check the new behavior

Closes #11236

issue_title: keycloak-x - unable to modify mssql/SQLServer port to anything other than 1433
issue_body:
### Describe the bug

Setting the KC_DB_URL_PORT environment variable does not work. The default of 1433 is retained when keycloak attempts to connect to the database.

### Version

17.0.1

### Expected behavior

Specifying the environment property  KC_DB_URL_PORT should be honored. Keycloak should try to establish it's database connections using that port.

### Actual behavior

Here is the error that shows up at startup:

` 2022-04-12 13:46:09,475 WARN  [org.hibernate.engine.jdbc.env.internal.JdbcEnvironmentInitiator] (JPA Startup Thread: keycloak-default) HHH000342: Could not obtain connection to query metadata: com.microsoft.sqlserver.jdbc.SQLServerException: The TCP/IP connection to the host my-mssql.db, port 1433 has failed. Error: "connect timed out. Verify the connection properties. Make sure that an instance of SQL Server is running on the host and accepting TCP/IP connections at the port. Make sure that TCP connections to the port are not blocked by a firewall.".
	at com.microsoft.sqlserver.jdbc.SQLServerException.makeFromDriverError(SQLServerException.java:234)
	at com.microsoft.sqlserver.jdbc.SQLServerException.ConvertConnectExceptionToSQLServerException(SQLServerException.java:285)
	at com.microsoft.sqlserver.jdbc.SocketFinder.findSocket(IOBuffer.java:2431)
	at com.microsoft.sqlserver.jdbc.TDSChannel.open(IOBuffer.java:656)
	at com.microsoft.sqlserver.jdbc.SQLServerConnection.connectHelper(SQLServerConnection.java:2440)
	at com.microsoft.sqlserver.jdbc.SQLServerConnection.login(SQLServerConnection.java:2103)
	at com.microsoft.sqlserver.jdbc.SQLServerConnection.connectInternal(SQLServerConnection.java:1950)
	at com.microsoft.sqlserver.jdbc.SQLServerConnection.connect(SQLServerConnection.java:1162)
	at com.microsoft.sqlserver.jdbc.SQLServerDriver.connect(SQLServerDriver.java:735)
	at io.agroal.pool.ConnectionFactory.createConnection(ConnectionFactory.java:210)
	at io.agroal.pool.ConnectionPool$CreateConnectionTask.call(ConnectionPool.java:513)
	at io.agroal.pool.ConnectionPool$CreateConnectionTask.call(ConnectionPool.java:494)
	at java.base/java.util.concurrent.FutureTask.run(FutureTask.java:264)
	at io.agroal.pool.util.PriorityScheduledExecutor.beforeExecute(PriorityScheduledExecutor.java:75)
	at java.base/java.util.concurrent.ThreadPoolExecutor.runWorker(ThreadPoolExecutor.java:1126)
	at java.base/java.util.concurrent.ThreadPoolExecutor$Worker.run(ThreadPoolExecutor.java:628)
	at java.base/java.lang.Thread.run(Thread.java:829)
`

### How to Reproduce?

Here is the relevant section of the chart yaml's **env** I am using to specify the database properties.


```
        env:
            - name: KC_DB
              value: mssql
            - name: KC_DB_URL_HOST
            - value: my-mssql.db
            - name: KC_DB_URL_PORT

```

```

### Anything else?

The dbchecker honors the property just fine. It appears that the port is hardcoded to 1433 in org.keycloak.quarkus.runtime.storage.database.Database. Reviewing that file suggests the problem would be true of other databases...

issue_title: 'Aggregate attribute values' mapper option not applied to group hierarchy
issue_body:
### Describe the bug

In a group hierarchy Multivalued attributes (with 'Aggregate attribute values' option set) are not properly aggregated into the Generated Access Token.

### Version

12.0.4

### Expected behavior

For a 'User Attribute' token claim that is Multivalued and has the 'Aggregate attribute values' option set, values found for a user's group should be combined with attribute values found for any ancestor of that group.

### Actual behavior

A parent group's attribute values are ignored instead of aggregated (even though inheritance works fine when the child group contains no values for the attribute).

### How to Reproduce?

1. Create a group, named A with attribute 'x' with value `A##1`
2. Create a group B, that is a child group of A, with attribute 'x' with value `B##2`
3. Create a user C that is a member of group B.
4. Make a User Attribute mapper for a User Attribute 'x' and Token Claim Name 'x' with the 'Multivalued' and 'Aggregate attribute values' options set.
5. Include the mapper in a Client Scope that is included to a client's "Assigned Default Client Scopes" and evaluate for user C.

The evaluation result ('Generated Access Token') includes claim 'x' with values ["B", "2"] (instead of the desired ["A", "1", "B", "2"]).

### Anything else?

This is [KEYCLOAK-17252](https://issues.redhat.com/browse/KEYCLOAK-17252) re-entered. 
It was closed as if outdated, but for us it still is and has always been a very relevant current issue (running with the provided PR in production).

The PR that fixes this issue: https://github.com/keycloak/keycloak/pull/7871
(after all this time only the added unit test causes a git conflict right now; we will address this).
commit_message:
Mapper option 'Aggregate attribute values' is now applied to group hierarchy (#7871)

Closes #11255

issue_title: Failed to list UMA policies when a user who was used in a policy got deleted.
issue_body:
### Describe the bug

The policy endpoint returns error response when a user who was used in a policy got deleted from the realm (see reproduce steps for more details)

This issue is critical as the user management is not under control of a resource server. It's realm administrator's task. Once it happens, the resource server is no longer able to manage policies anymore.  

### Version

17.0.1

### Expected behavior

At minimal, deleting users should not affect the policy endpoint. The list policy endpoint should still work. Ideally, when a user is deleted, all policies should be updated to reflect the changes.

### Actual behavior

When a user used in a policy was deleted from the realm, list all policy endpoint stop working. It returned unknown error response. 

### How to Reproduce?

1) create a test realm
	login to Administration Console, Add realm "Test"
	
2) add test client
	select the "Test" realm, go to "Clients" -> "Create" and select the attached client json file "bug-test" (see Anything else), and "Save".
	
3) add a user "testuser"
	go to "Users", add a user "testuser"
	
4) register a protected resource
	get a client token (replace hostname:port in the curl requests)
	$curl --location --request POST 'http://hostname:port/auth/realms/Test/protocol/openid-connect/token' \
       --header 'Content-Type: application/x-www-form-urlencoded' \
       --data-urlencode 'grant_type=client_credentials' \
       --data-urlencode 'client_id=bug-test' \
       --data-urlencode 'client_secret=KlCEOISCILLXARITyE09zfo9wnOxAlci'
	   
	register a protected resource "test-protected-resource" (replace the <access_token> in the request with the one got above)
	curl --location --request POST 'http://hostname:port/auth/realms/Test/authz/protection/resource_set' \
       --header 'Content-Type: application/json' \
       --header 'Authorization: Bearer <access_token>' \
       --data-raw '{
           "name": "test-protected-resource",
           "ownerManagedAccess": true,
           "scopes": ["read", "write"]
       }'
	   
	write down the resource id (_id property in the response).
	
5) add an UMA policy
	add an UMA policy with the user "testuser" to the resource created above (replace the <resource_id>)
	curl --location --request POST 'http://hostname:port/auth/realms/Test/authz/protection/uma-policy/<resource_id>' \
       --header 'Authorization: Bearer <access token>' \
       --header 'Content-Type: application/json' \
       --data-raw '{
               "name": "test-policy",
               "decisionStrategy": "AFFIRMATIVE",
               "scopes": ["read"],
               "users": ["testuser"]
       }'
	write down the policy id (id property in the response).
	
6) query policy
	search all policies
	curl --location --request GET 'http://hostname:port/auth/realms/Test/authz/protection/uma-policy' \
		--header 'Authorization: Bearer <access token>'
	
	you should see one policy in response.
	
7) reproduce the bug by deleteing the user "testuser" from Administration Console
	login to Administration Console, go to "Users", and delete the user "testuser" in "Test" realm.
	search all policies again,
	
	curl --location --request GET 'http://hostname:port/auth/realms/Test/authz/protection/uma-policy' \
		--header 'Authorization: Bearer <access token>'

	you will get an error response "{"error":"unknown_error"}". keycloak server log shows a NullPointerException thrown.
	

### Anything else?

bug-test.json

```
{
    "clientId": "bug-test",
	"secret": "KlCEOISCILLXARITyE09zfo9wnOxAlci",
    "enabled": true,

        "*"
    ],

	},

    "protocol": "openid-connect"
}
```

A quick fix could be:

```
 .../policy/provider/permission/UMAPolicyProviderFactory.java        | 6 +++++-
 1 file changed, 5 insertions(+), 1 deletion(-)

diff --git a/authz/policy/common/src/main/java/org/keycloak/authorization/policy/provider/permission/UMAPolicyProviderFactory.java b/authz/policy/common/src/main/java/org/keycloak/authorization/policy/provider/permission/UMAPolicyProviderFactory.java
index 51107ae8a2..db0daec628 100644
--- a/authz/policy/common/src/main/java/org/keycloak/authorization/policy/provider/permission/UMAPolicyProviderFactory.java
+++ b/authz/policy/common/src/main/java/org/keycloak/authorization/policy/provider/permission/UMAPolicyProviderFactory.java
@@ -33,6 +33,7 @@ import org.keycloak.models.KeycloakSession;
 import org.keycloak.models.KeycloakSessionFactory;
 import org.keycloak.models.RealmModel;
 import org.keycloak.models.RoleModel;
+import org.keycloak.models.UserModel;
 import org.keycloak.models.utils.KeycloakModelUtils;
 import org.keycloak.models.utils.ModelToRepresentation;
 import org.keycloak.models.utils.RepresentationToModel;
@@ -349,7 +350,10 @@ public class UMAPolicyProviderFactory implements PolicyProviderFactory<UmaPermis
                 UserPolicyRepresentation rep = UserPolicyRepresentation.class.cast(associatedRep);
 
                 for (String user : rep.getUsers()) {
-                    representation.addUser(authorization.getKeycloakSession().users().getUserById(realm, user).getUsername());
+                    UserModel userModel = authorization.getKeycloakSession().users().getUserById(realm, user);
+                    if (userModel != null) {
+                        representation.addUser(userModel.getUsername());
+                    }
                 }

issue_title: CVE-2021-42392 - H2 console vulnerable to Remote Code Execution (RCE)
issue_body:
## Summary

[com.h2database:h2](http://www.h2database.com/html/main.html) is a database engine

Affected versions of this package are vulnerable to Remote Code Execution (RCE). H2 Console allows loading of custom classes from remote servers through JNDI. This can lead to code execution

If remote access was enabled explicitly and some protection method (such as security constraint) are not set, an intruder can load their own custom class and execute their code in a process using H2 Console (a H2 Server process or a web server with H2 Console servlet).

_Note:_ It should be noted that H2 Console doesn't accept remote connections by default.

## Version

18.0.0 or higher

## Impact

Low. Keycloak is not vulnerable to this issue and does not enable it by default. H2 database is used only for development purposes and testing. We do not recommend its usage in production.

Below you can find more details:

Adding the quote from the CVE for more context: "_H2 Console doesn't accept remote connections by default. If remote access was enabled explicitly and some protection method (such as security constraint) wasn't set, an intruder can load own custom class and execute its code in a process with H2 Console (H2 Server process or a web server with H2 Console servlet)._" - [https://github.com/h2database/h2database/security/advisories/GHSA-h376-j262-vhq6](https://github.com/h2database/h2database/security/advisories/GHSA-h376-j262-vhq6)

## Remediation

Upgrade `com.h2database:h2` to version 2.0.206 or higher.

## References

-   [GItHub Advisory](https://github.com/h2database/h2database/security/advisories/GHSA-h376-j262-vhq6)
-   [GitHub Commit](https://github.com/h2database/h2database/commit/b24aa46f48904ce64443f8f4353d70a2eed09037)
-   [The JNDI Strikes Back – Unauthenticated RCE in H2 Database Console](https://jfrog.com/blog/the-jndi-strikes-back-unauthenticated-rce-in-h2-database-console/)
-  https://github.com/keycloak/keycloak/issues/12607

## Additional information

Keycloak is **not** vulnerable, but it is affected considering the usage of _h2database_ dependency in the WildFly legacy and Quarkus distributions.

Before updating H2 on Keycloak, first it is necessary to WildFly and Quarkus directly. Until now, Quarkus 2.8.0.Final and WildFly 26 both are still on version 1.4.197 of H2.

The Quarkus team updated the dependency in the main branch, although, we need to wait for the upcoming releases.

## Credits

- https://security.snyk.io/vuln/SNYK-JAVA-COMH2DATABASE-2331071

issue_title: kc.sh, kcadm.sh, kcreg.sh do not respect environment variables JAVA, JAVA_HOME to select JVM
issue_body:
### Description

When executing Keycloak Quarkus [`bin/kc.sh`](https://github.com/keycloak/keycloak/blob/main/quarkus/dist/src/main/content/bin/kc.sh), [`bin/kcadm.sh`](https://github.com/keycloak/keycloak/blob/main/integration/client-cli/admin-cli/src/main/bin/kcadm.sh), [`bin/kcreg.sh`](https://github.com/keycloak/keycloak/blob/main/integration/client-cli/client-registration-cli/src/main/bin/kcreg.sh), the first `java` command found in `$PATH` is executed. The environment variables `JAVA` and `JAVA_HOME` are not available to specify the `java` command / JVM to execute.

Our default server image needs to have a Java 8 JVM configured as "default" JVM in `$PATH`. Running Keycloak Quarkus `kc.sh` results in a UnsupportedClassVersionError because the default Java 8 JVM is used.

```
$ java -version
java version "1.8.0_181"
Java(TM) SE Runtime Environment (build 1.8.0_181-b13)
Java HotSpot(TM) 64-Bit Server VM (build 25.181-b13, mixed mode)

$ ./bin/kc.sh
Error: A JNI error has occurred, please check your installation and try again
Exception in thread "main" java.lang.UnsupportedClassVersionError: io/quarkus/bootstrap/runner/QuarkusEntryPoint has been compiled by a more recent version of the Java Runtime (class file version 55.0), this version of the Java Runtime only recognizes class file versions up to 52.0
	at java.lang.ClassLoader.defineClass1(Native Method)
	at java.lang.ClassLoader.defineClass(ClassLoader.java:763)
	at java.security.SecureClassLoader.defineClass(SecureClassLoader.java:142)
	at java.net.URLClassLoader.defineClass(URLClassLoader.java:467)
	at java.net.URLClassLoader.access$100(URLClassLoader.java:73)
	at java.net.URLClassLoader$1.run(URLClassLoader.java:368)
	at java.net.URLClassLoader$1.run(URLClassLoader.java:362)
	at java.security.AccessController.doPrivileged(Native Method)
	at java.net.URLClassLoader.findClass(URLClassLoader.java:361)
	at java.lang.ClassLoader.loadClass(ClassLoader.java:424)
	at sun.misc.Launcher$AppClassLoader.loadClass(Launcher.java:349)
	at java.lang.ClassLoader.loadClass(ClassLoader.java:357)
	at sun.launcher.LauncherHelper.checkAndLoadMain(LauncherHelper.java:495)
```

With `JAVA_HOME` is set to the installation directory of a Java 16 JVM (as example), `bin/kc.sh` still executes our default Java 8 JVM.

```
$ /usr/lib/jvm/java-16-openjdk-amd64/bin/java -version
openjdk version "16.0.1" 2021-04-20
OpenJDK Runtime Environment (build 16.0.1+9-Ubuntu-120.04)
OpenJDK 64-Bit Server VM (build 16.0.1+9-Ubuntu-120.04, mixed mode, sharing)
dev in dschoemer in ~/development/keycloak-17.0.1
$ JAVA_HOME=/usr/lib/jvm/java-16-openjdk-amd64 ./bin/kc.sh
Error: A JNI error has occurred, please check your installation and try again
Exception in thread "main" java.lang.UnsupportedClassVersionError: io/quarkus/bootstrap/runner/QuarkusEntryPoint has been compiled by a more recent version of the Java Runtime (class file version 55.0), this version of the Java Runtime only recognizes class file versions up to 52.0
	at java.lang.ClassLoader.defineClass1(Native Method)
	at java.lang.ClassLoader.defineClass(ClassLoader.java:763)
	at java.security.SecureClassLoader.defineClass(SecureClassLoader.java:142)
	at java.net.URLClassLoader.defineClass(URLClassLoader.java:467)
	at java.net.URLClassLoader.access$100(URLClassLoader.java:73)
	at java.net.URLClassLoader$1.run(URLClassLoader.java:368)
	at java.net.URLClassLoader$1.run(URLClassLoader.java:362)
	at java.security.AccessController.doPrivileged(Native Method)
	at java.net.URLClassLoader.findClass(URLClassLoader.java:361)
	at java.lang.ClassLoader.loadClass(ClassLoader.java:424)
	at sun.misc.Launcher$AppClassLoader.loadClass(Launcher.java:349)
	at java.lang.ClassLoader.loadClass(ClassLoader.java:357)
	at sun.launcher.LauncherHelper.checkAndLoadMain(LauncherHelper.java:495)
```

### Expected behavior

`bin/kc.sh` (`bin/kcadm.sh` and `bin/kcreg.sh`, too) respect environment variables `JAVA` and/or `JAVA_HOME` to start a selected JVM.

### Actual behavior

`bin/kc.sh` (`bin/kcadm.sh` and `bin/kcreg.sh`, too) start the JVM whose `java` command is the first one found in environment variable `PATH`.

### Steps to reproduce

1. Set default JVM to a JVM older than Java 11
2. Verify that `java` starts the old JVM
3. Set environment variable `JAVA` or `JAVA_HOME` to a Java 11 JVM (or newer)
   1. Set `JAVA` to the fully qualified path of a JVM 11 (or newer) `java` command
   2. or set `JAVA_HOME` to the fully qualified installation directory of a JVM 11 (or newer)
4. Execute `bin/kc.sh`

### Discussion

_No response_

### Motivation

Keycloak Legacy supports environment variables `JAVA` and `JAVA_HOME`.
Keycloak `bin/kc.bat` supports environment variables `JAVA` and `JAVA_HOME`.

### Details

I will prepare a PR to update [`bin/kcadm.sh`](https://github.com/keycloak/keycloak/blob/main/integration/client-cli/admin-cli/src/main/bin/kcadm.sh), [`bin/kcreg.sh`](https://github.com/keycloak/keycloak/blob/main/integration/client-cli/client-registration-cli/src/main/bin/kcreg.sh) and [`bin/kc.sh`](https://github.com/keycloak/keycloak/blob/main/quarkus/dist/src/main/content/bin/kc.sh) to support environment variables `JAVA` and `JAVA_HOME`.
commit_message:
Update kc/kcadm/kcreg.sh to support JAVA and JAVA_HOME (#11337)

When executing Keycloak Quarkus `bin/kc.sh`, `bin/kcadm.sh`, `bin/kcreg.sh`,
the first java command found in `$PATH` is executed. The environment
variables `JAVA` and `JAVA_HOME` are not available to specify the java
command / JVM to execute.

This commit updates `bin/kc.sh`, `bin/kcadm.sh`, and `bin/kcreg.sh` to support
environment variables `JAVA` and `JAVA_HOME`.

Resolves #11336

issue_title: authentication checks cause 'Cookie not found' error
issue_body:
### Describe the bug

When a user doesn't have credentials, my app occasionally checks auth status in case the user authenticated externally. If this check happens while a user has the login page open, the user won't be able to complete authentication in that login page, with error: `Cookie not found. Please make sure cookies are enabled in your browser.`.

I propose that the cookie not be cleared if `response_type=none`.

### Version

17.0.1

### How to Reproduce?

- in tab 1, open auth page: `/auth/realms/my-realm/protocol/openid-connect/auth?response_type=code&client_id=my-client&state=abc1234&scope=profile&code_challenge=def5678&code_challenge_method=S256&redirect_uri=https%3A%2F%2Fexample.com%2Fsignin%3Faction%3Dclose`
- in tab 2, to simulate an authentication check, open the following: `/auth/realms/my-realm/protocol/openid-connect/auth?prompt=none&response_type=none&client_id=my-client&code_challenge=def5678&code_challenge_method=S256&redirect_uri=https%3A%2F%2Fexample.com%2Fsignin%3Faction%3dmessage`. If this redirects to `redirect_uri` with `error=login_required`, this tells us the user isn't authenticated. Note the `prompt=none` and `response_type=none`.
- return to tab 1 and complete authentication, and see error `Cookie not found. Please make sure cookies are enabled in your browser.`

issue_title: ID token encryption without specifying id_token_encrypted_response_enc does not follow OIDC Dynamic Client Registration specification
issue_body:
### Describe the bug

According to [OpenID Connect Dynamic Client Registration 1.0](https://openid.net/specs/openid-connect-registration-1_0.html#ClientMetadata), the following patterns can support encryption.
1. both `id_token_encrypted_response_alg` and `id_token_encrypted_response_enc` are specified.
2. `id_token_encrypted_response_alg` is specified while `id_token_encrypted_response_enc` is not specified.

For the pattern 2, the default value of `id_token_encrypted_response_enc` is A128CBC-HS256.
However, the current keycloak does not encrypt ID token in the patter 2.

### Version

17.0.1

### Expected behavior

For the pattern 2, the default value of `id_token_encrypted_response_enc` is A128CBC-HS256.

### Actual behavior

The current keycloak does not encrypt ID token in the patter 2.

### How to Reproduce?

In client settings, set a valid algorithm to `id_token_encrypted_response_alg` but does not set an algorithm to `id_token_encrypted_response_enc`.

### Anything else?

_No response_

Closes #11392

issue_title: Fix Device Authorization Grant error responses returned as HTML instead of JSON
issue_body:
### Describe the bug

While testing the OAuth 2.0 Device Authorization Grant Key for a client without the device grant enabled, Keycloak returns the following error:

> Client is not allowed to initiate OAuth 2.0 Device Authorization Grant. The flow is disabled for the client.

Returning an error is correct but the error message is returned as an html document, instead of json. The same happens for other errors.

From https://datatracker.ietf.org/doc/html/rfc8628#section-3.2:

> In the event of an error (such as an invalidly configured client),
>  the authorization server responds in the same way as the token
>  endpoint specified in 
>  Section 5.2 of [RFC6749](https://datatracker.ietf.org/doc/html/rfc6749#section-5.2).

From https://datatracker.ietf.org/doc/html/rfc6749#section-5.2:

> The parameters are included in the entity-body of the HTTP response
>  using the "application/json" media type as defined by [RFC4627]. The
>  parameters are serialized into a JSON structure by adding each
>  parameter at the highest structure level. Parameter names and string
>  values are included as JSON strings. Numerical values are included
>  as JSON numbers. The order of parameters does not matter and can
>  vary.


### Version

16.1.0

### Expected behavior

Return json error in the format described in [section 5.2 of OAuth documentation](https://datatracker.ietf.org/doc/html/rfc6749#section-5.2).
Keycloak has ErrorResponseException for this.

### Actual behavior

Return html page.

### How to Reproduce?

F.e. disable OAuth 2.0 Device Authorization Grant for a client and try to execute Device Authorization Grant.

### Anything else?

_No response_

issue_title: Cannot use a nested claim value for Regex comparison in a Regex Policy in Authorization Services
issue_body:
### Describe the bug

If I add a custom mapper in my client to put in a user attribute such that my JWT access token contains a section like this:

```json
{
  "userinfo": {
      "tenant": "abc"
  }
}
```

and then I go and create a regex policy for comparing this value with a Target Claim `userinfo.tenant` and regex pattern `^abc$` ,  then the policy still always evaluates to false.

I'm guessing this is because the regex policy code is trying to find an attribute `{"userinfo.tenant": "abc"}` kind of value in the access token.

I also tried putting the target claim as `userinfo[tenant]`, `userinfo##tenant` and `userinfo\.tenant` but none of them worked.

What WORKED was not nesting the `tenant` key-value pair and changing the regex policy to read a non nested value.


Either this is bug, or I'm missing a piece of documentation that mentions how to read nested claims in the regex policy thing.

Could someone please confirm the issue or suggest a fix ?




### Version

17.0.1

### Expected behavior

The nested claim value can be read and the regex comparision works properly for valid values.

### Actual behavior

Nested claims are not working in regex policy

### How to Reproduce?

Create a regex policy in Authorization services that tries to compare with a nested claim value.

### Anything else?

_No response_

issue_title: Wrong encoding used for container
issue_body:
After running Keycloak in containers, we noticed that the System Encoding is set to `ANSI_X3.4-1968` - however, if I were to run it locally using the source code and not a container, I see UTF-8.

The way I understand this can come about is it is derived from the system locale (on RHEL based systems there's a script that is often sourced - `/etc/profile.d/lang.sh` ) and the `ANSI_X3.4-1968` value can happen when there isn't a locale set (default).

I am curious as to what impacts this could have
- Are there possibly edge cases or errors that could come up? 
- Since the `ANSI_X3.4-1968` charset is basically ASCII, that could mean there are potentially issues for localization?
- Is it possible that this value is ignored and doesn't matter based on how Keycloak works?
- If I update my container to actually set UTF-8, would one expect problems due to the switch?
- Is there some instruction I am missing that we _should_ be setting the `file.encoding` Java system property manually?

EDIT: 2022-04-20

Looking closer, it appears this was actually handled in the "legacy" Keycloak image (Wildfly distribution) - https://github.com/keycloak/keycloak-containers/blob/main/server/Dockerfile#L12

Running the legacy image I was able to confirm that UTF-8 is reflected on the Server Info page.

`docker run -p 8080:8080 -e KEYCLOAK_USER=admin -e KEYCLOAK_PASSWORD=admin -it quay.io/keycloak/keycloak:17.0.1-legacy`

```
User Locale 	us_EN
System Encoding 	UTF-8
```

I do not see any of the similar fixes applied in the quarkus image build:
https://github.com/keycloak/keycloak/blob/main/quarkus/container/Dockerfile

And running that image I could confirm the `ANSI_X3.4-1968` value is set.

`docker run -p 8080:8080 -e KEYCLOAK_ADMIN=admin -e KEYCLOAK_ADMIN_PASSWORD=admin quay.io/keycloak/keycloak:17.0.1 start-dev`

```
User Locale 	us_EN
System Encoding 	ANSI_X3.4-1968
```


And while not purely a Keycloak concern, it seems that RHSSO image is in similar shape, where the locale value is using the default - https://github.com/jboss-container-images/redhat-sso-7-openshift-image/blob/sso75-dev/image.yaml

`docker run -p 8080:8080 -e SSO_ADMIN_USERNAME="admin" -e SSO_ADMIN_PASSWORD="admin" -it registry.redhat.io/rh-sso-7/sso75-openshift-rhel8`

```
User Locale 	us_EN
System Encoding 	ANSI_X3.4-1968
```

_Originally posted by @jsorah in https://github.com/keycloak/keycloak/discussions/11527_


-----
Expected behaviour: UTF-8 is used as it was in wildfly container and is used in general everywhere to avoid unwanted side effects (e.g. non-ascii emails).

As per @jsorah we should add
```
RUN  microdnf install glibc-langpack-en; 
ENV LANG=en_US.utf8
```
to the dockerfile. Will test it locally and create a PR if successfull.
